items.title: Perplexity Computer keeps stretching from research into end-to-end task execution
items.summary: Perplexity's push into tasks like tax prep shows the company wants Computer to be seen as an action-taking agent, not just a better search box.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the market is rewarding products that can actually complete work, not only summarize it.

items.title: OpenAI's Illinois liability push shows regulation is now a product-adjacent battle
items.summary: OpenAI's support for an Illinois liability bill highlights how legal definitions around AI responsibility are becoming a meaningful competitive issue for major labs.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: regulation is no longer outside the product story; it directly shapes deployment risk.

items.title: OpenAI's $122B funding round reset expectations for frontier-AI scale
items.summary: OpenAI's enormous raise reinforces that ChatGPT's commercial position is now influencing how the entire market thinks about compute, infrastructure, and capital requirements.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: funding scale now shapes which labs can keep pushing expensive frontier products.

items.title: Grok expands inside X with translation and photo-editing features
items.summary: X is widening Grok's usefulness with built-in translation and image-editing tools, reinforcing its strategy of making the assistant feel native to the platform rather than separate from it.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Grok's scale advantage comes from distribution through X, not just model branding.

items.title: Claude Cowork reaches general availability on desktop for enterprise teams
items.summary: Anthropic moved Claude Cowork to general availability on macOS and Windows, adding usage analytics, OpenTelemetry support, and tighter enterprise controls around rollout.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Anthropic is turning Claude from a strong assistant into a more governable workplace product with adoption tracking and admin controls.

items.title: Claude Code's leak turned Anthropic's harness into a public design reference
items.summary: The Claude Code source leak made the orchestration layer behind one of the hottest coding agents unusually legible, giving the market a clearer look at how serious agent harnesses are being built.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the AI coding race increasingly looks like a harness race, not just a model race.

items.title: Project Glasswing gives defenders early access to Anthropic's unreleased cyber model
items.summary: Anthropic is using Project Glasswing to put Mythos Preview in defenders' hands first, signaling a more security-first release strategy for high-risk Claude capabilities.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Claude's strongest upcoming capabilities are being framed as a deployment-governance problem, not just a product launch.

items.title: Meta launches Muse Spark to upgrade Meta AI across apps and API preview
items.summary: Meta introduced Muse Spark as the first model from Meta Superintelligence Labs, using it to power faster multimodal Meta AI experiences and a private preview API for partners.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Meta is tying a new flagship model directly to consumer distribution across WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and AI glasses.

items.title: Google AI Studio gets a stronger developer story when Gemma and Gemini move together
items.summary: Gemma 4 gives Google AI Studio more open-model credibility, making Google's stack feel broader than a single closed Gemini endpoint for developers and prototype teams.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the most durable platforms usually win by offering multiple lanes, not one monolithic model.

items.title: GitHub Copilot expands agent control and browser debugging in VS Code
items.summary: GitHub's March releases for Copilot in VS Code added Autopilot sessions, finer agent permissions, integrated browser debugging, and richer media support inside chat.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: GitHub is pushing Copilot further into full-session autonomous development instead of keeping it as a narrow inline completion tool.

items.title: DeepL is broadening from translation into agent-style enterprise assistance
items.summary: DeepL's AI Agent positioning suggests the company wants to own more of the multilingual workflow stack, not just the final translation step.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: specialized AI products can still grow by expanding around one trusted core job.

items.title: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google align more openly against model-copying pressure
items.summary: The leading labs are taking a harder public line on distillation and model copying, a sign that open-model pressure from players like DeepSeek is reshaping the rules of competition.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: open-model competition is now triggering strategy changes inside the biggest closed-model labs.

items.title: Perplexity's reported ARR momentum suggests research products can still break out
items.summary: Reported revenue momentum is reinforcing Perplexity's shift from interesting search startup to one of the few serious consumer AI products with clear commercial pull.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: revenue traction matters because it shows users are paying for a research-native alternative to the biggest general assistants.

items.title: OpenAI's policy paper reads like a mini-New Deal for the AI economy
items.summary: OpenAI is no longer speaking only as a lab shipping ChatGPT upgrades, but as a company actively trying to shape how governments respond to large-scale AI disruption.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI policy is becoming a direct extension of platform strategy.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic are being discussed more like future IPO companies than labs
items.summary: Reporting around IPO pressure shows how quickly the frontier-model race is being financialized as ChatGPT and Claude keep scaling revenue and compute spend at the same time.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the market is increasingly evaluating AI leaders as giant infrastructure businesses, not just products.

items.title: Anthropic's huge TPU deal shows Claude's growth is now gated by infrastructure scale
items.summary: Anthropic's expanded compute arrangement with Google and Broadcom makes clear that serving and training Claude at the frontier is now as much an infrastructure story as a model story.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: demand for top assistants is increasingly constrained by how much compute can actually be delivered.

items.title: Figma opens its canvas to AI agents with an MCP server beta
items.summary: Figma now lets AI agents create and edit design assets on the canvas through its MCP server beta, with skills used to encode design-system rules and workflow context.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: design tools are becoming part of the agent loop, not just a handoff destination after code is written.

items.title: ChatGPT adds a File Library for saved uploads and generated assets
items.summary: OpenAI added a persistent Library in ChatGPT so uploaded files and generated assets can be reused later, with recent-file insertion and sidebar browsing across web and mobile surfaces.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: persistent files make ChatGPT feel more like an ongoing workspace than a disposable chat window.
